name: Edna Lewis' Thirteen-Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Dried black beans, washed and picked over
2 cups Packaged 13-bean soup mix, washed and picked over
1 lb Smoked pork shoulder or meaty ham bone
1 cup Fresh or canned tomatoes, peeled, seeded, and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large kettle, cover beans with cold water. Refrigerate and soak overnight.
2. The next day, drain the beans, add pork or ham bone, onion, thyme, bay leaf, pepper, and cold water. Place over medium-high heat and bring to a simmer.
3. Reduce heat and maintain a simmer for 2 1/2 hours.
4. Remove onion, ham bone, and fat; cut up meat into small pieces. Season with salt and pepper as needed.
5. Add tomatoes and olive oil. Put half the mixture in a blender and blend until almost smooth but not liquified.
6. Add to the whole bean mixture. To serve, stir in sherry and reheat. Ladle into warm soup bowls.
7. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
